See what's free at AOL.com. 
Sorry about the enclosure. The program can and will run efficiently on my little IBM Think Pad computer as it did in the past when I used it in my old company. This software is in use in many, places. Bill used it at Wright Ryan and Ledgewood in Maine . Reed & Reed uses it. The consulting firm which we used for scheduling and estimating used it, Brower, Kriz & Stynchcomb and so on. I can give you many references for the system. There is IT support and service out of Puerto Rico if we have a problem. I have sent a web site reference by separate e mail. 
 
Lafayette Contractors LLC
6100 Red Hook Quarter, Suite A2-7
St. Thomas, VI 00802
Tel: 340 779-8054
Fax:340 779-8055
Cell:340 690-7194



From: CMi3540012@aol.com [mailto:CMi3540012@aol.com] 
Sent: Wednesday, March 28, 2007 5:49 PM
To: john_amerling@earthlink.net
Cc: CMi3540012@aol.com; w_rowles@yahoo.com
Subject: Fwd: Timberline Proposal
 
John,
 
The hard quote you refer to is not attached.
 
Please provide.
 
Are the computers we have able to efficiently run this program with out additional modifications or costs?
 
Is there a web site you can give me that gives information on the program? 
 
Charles
